description: Automatically detects and renders vertical, horizontal, and slant asymptotes of a plot
utility: Visualizes asymptotic behavior of functions with automatic detection and optional animation

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph to draw asymptotes on

arguments.name: plot
arguments.description: The plot expression to analyze for asymptotes

arguments.name: startX
arguments.description: Optional starting X position for trace animation

alternates.description: Auto-detect all asymptote types (vertical, horizontal, slant)

alternates.syntax: asymptote(graph, plot, dash()), or asymptote(graph, plot, c(blue))
alternates.description: Apply custom dash pattern or color styling
Specify X position for trace animation

examples.scenario: Asymptotes of 1/x with custom stroke width on a plotting graph
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,7), 21.5, 32.4, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
plot_1=plot(graph_1,"1/(x)",range(-5,5))
asymptote_1=asymptote(graph_1,plot_1,s(5))

examples.scenario: Asymptotes alongside tangent and normal on same plot
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,7), 21.5, 32.4, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
plot_1=plot(graph_1,"1/(x)",range(-5,5))
tangent_1 = tangent(graph_1, plot_1, 1)
normal_1 = normal(graph_1, plot_1, 1)
asymptote_1=asymptote(graph_1,plot_1,s(5))

examples.scenario: Detect asymptotes of a rational function
examples.expression:
G = g2d(-5, 5, -10, 10)
f = plot(G, "1/(x-2)")
asymptote(G, f)

G = g2d(-10, 10, -10, 10)
f = plot(G, "1/x")
asymptote(G, f, dash(5))
